intro: Your class must organize a science exhibition in two weeks.
objective: Plan exhibits, budget, team roles, and handle surprises.

nodes.text: First, how do you divide the team?

choices.title: Assign roles based on strengths
choices.description: Let each member choose what they do best.

choices.title: You decide everything
choices.description: Take full control to save time.

nodes.text: With a week left, you are over budget. What now?
The day before, a teammate falls sick.
One day left. How do you cover the missing work?

choices.title: Simplify the exhibit
choices.description: Reduce scope and focus on essentials.

choices.title: Ignore it and present as-is
choices.description: Hope the missing part goes unnoticed.

nodes.text: The exhibition is complete. The crowd learns. The team learns. You learn.
reflection: How did early role choices affect the final crisis?
What was the most important preparation step?

intro: You are planning a one-day educational trip with friends.
objective: Plan transport, food, budget, and a backup plan.

nodes.text: Transport choice:

choices.title: Public bus with backup route
choices.description: Cheap and reliable, with a backup plan.

nodes.text: It is hot midday. How do you manage food and hydration?

choices.title: Buy food at the destination
choices.description: Less to carry, but unpredictable cost.

nodes.text: Unexpected heavy rain in the afternoon.
Rain continues. What is your next move?

choices.title: Activate shelter plan
choices.description: Move to the pre-identified covered area.

nodes.text: You return home. Everyone is safe. Some plans changed. That is planning.
reflection: Why did the backup plan matter more than the main plan?
What would you pack differently next time?

intro: You are given responsibility for the school garden for one month.
objective: Balance watering, weeding, planting, and pest control.

nodes.text: Week 1: How do you start?
Week 2: Pests appear. What do you do?
A heatwave hits in Week 3. Water supply is limited.
Water is limited. How do you save the garden?

choices.title: Water everything equally
choices.description: Equal share, but may not be enough.

nodes.text: The garden survives. Some plants thrive. Some fade. You learned what a month teaches.
reflection: How did early observation help in the crisis?
What is the cost of quick fixes?

intro: You have a monthly allowance and real expenses ahead.
objective: Survive the month with health, studies, and friendships intact.

nodes.text: Week 1: You receive allowance. First action?

choices.title: Spend a little on celebration first
Keep everything in pocket and decide daily

nodes.text: Mid-month: A friend asks for a loan. What do you do?
Unexpected: School fees notice arrives for a small activity.
Last week: You are short. How do you finish?
The month ends. Some money remains. Some lessons remain.
reflection: What was the most important budget decision?
How does planning early reduce crisis?

intro: Your family is hosting a small festival event at home.
objective: Coordinate tasks, budget, guests, and handle last-minute changes.

nodes.text: Three days before: What do you prepare first?

choices.title: Guest list and food plan
choices.description: Plan who comes and what is cooked.

choices.title: Wait and do everything on the day

nodes.text: Day before: A key ingredient is not available. What now?

choices.title: Substitute smartly
choices.description: Use an alternative dish with available items.

nodes.text: Festival day: More guests arrive than expected.
Extra guests are here. What do you do?

choices.title: Serve smaller portions and share joy

nodes.text: The festival is complete. Music fades. Guests leave. The house is warm.
reflection: How did preparation affect the unexpected guest moment?
What is the real purpose of celebration?

intro: You are sent to buy groceries for the family with a list and a budget.
objective: Buy everything, stay within budget, and handle store surprises.

nodes.text: At the store: Some items are more expensive than expected.
You see a big discount on a non-essential item your sibling wants.

choices.title: Buy it as a gift
choices.description: Surprise your sibling, but spend extra.

choices.title: Buy it but ask for extra money later

nodes.text: Carrying heavy bags, the handle breaks halfway.
Bags are heavy and one broke. How do you reach home?

choices.title: Rest and redistribute weight
choices.description: Take a break, then continue smartly.

nodes.text: You reach home. The list is mostly complete. The family eats. You learn.
reflection: When is flexibility better than strict discipline?
How do surprises test preparation?

intro: You have two weeks to build a working model for the science fair.
objective: Research, build, test, and present. Manage time and failures.

nodes.text: Week 1: How do you start research?
Week 2: Your first prototype fails completely.

choices.title: Analyze failure, rebuild
choices.description: Study what went wrong and fix it.

nodes.text: The night before: Your display board tears.
Display board is damaged. One night left.

choices.title: Ignore the board and focus on speech

nodes.text: The fair opens. Your model works. Your board tells a story. Your voice carries it.
reflection: How did failure improve the final result?
What mattered more: the model or the explanation?

intro: You are organizing a surprise birthday for a close friend.
objective: Plan guest list, food, games, and keep the secret.

nodes.text: Guest list: Who do you invite?
The friend almost discovers the plan. What do you do?

choices.title: Cancel the surprise, tell them
choices.description: Lose the surprise, keep the event.

choices.title: Lie completely
choices.description: Make up an elaborate false story.

nodes.text: Party day: The cake delivery is late.
Cake is late. Guests are arriving.

choices.title: Start games and activities first
choices.description: Delay cake cutting, keep energy high.

choices.title: Send someone to pick it up

nodes.text: The friend is surprised. The room is warm. The secret was worth it.
reflection: What was the hardest part to plan?
How did secrecy change the joy?

intro: You are preparing for an inter-school sports day in two weeks.
objective: Balance training, rest, diet, and equipment.

nodes.text: Training plan: How do you practice?
You need better shoes but have limited budget.
Three days before: You catch a mild cold.
You have a mild cold. Sports day is close.
The sports day arrives. Your body is ready or not. Your mind is ready.
reflection: What is the difference between preparation and obsession?
How did rest contribute to performance?

intro: You are leading a small team to clean a local park.
objective: Organize people, tools, safety, and complete the area.

nodes.text: Team arrives. How do you start?

choices.title: Map the area and divide zones

nodes.text: Midway: Some volunteers are tired and want to quit.

choices.title: Let them leave, finish with fewer

nodes.text: You find a broken glass bottle near a child playing.
Hazard found: sharp glass near children.

choices.title: Tell someone else to handle it

nodes.text: The park is cleaner. The children are safer. The team is stronger.
reflection: How did leadership show in small moments?
What is the long-term effect of a clean shared space?
